hero.headline: DPU-COL Online MBA in Marketing Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #41 University 2025 | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹1,89,400

sections.tldr: DPU-COL Online MBA in Marketing Management covers consumer behaviour, marketing research, sales and distribution management, product and brand management, retail management, marketing of services, international marketing, and integrated marketing communication. NAAC A++ NIRF #41. Total fee ₹1,89,400 with ₹10,000 discount. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. edX certifications bundled. UGC-DEB approved.

about.body:
DPU-COL's MBA in Marketing Management has one of the most comprehensive marketing curricula in the online MBA segment. Semester 3 has seven subjects (compared to four in most DPU specs): consumer behaviour, marketing research, sales and distribution management, product and brand management, retail management, marketing of services, and a generic elective. Semester 4 adds international marketing, IMC, another elective, and project work.

The curriculum covers both B2C marketing (consumer behaviour, brand management, retail) and B2B marketing (sales and distribution, marketing of services). The inclusion of retail management and marketing of services as standalone subjects means graduates understand both product and service marketing at depth, not just as modules within a general marketing course.

Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune holds NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 (University 2025). edX certifications are bundled. At ₹1,89,400, the programme is mid-range for a NAAC A++ marketing MBA with this subject depth.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Marketing Management specialisation subjects. This spec has more subjects per semester than most DPU specialisations.

subjects.name: Consumer Behavior
subjects.desc: Consumer decision-making models, psychological and sociological factors, perception and attitudes, cultural influences, and digital consumer behaviour patterns.

subjects.name: Marketing Research
subjects.desc: Research design, qualitative and quantitative methods, questionnaire design, sampling techniques, data analysis and interpretation, and marketing research report preparation.

subjects.name: Sales and Distribution Management
subjects.desc: Sales force structure design, territory management, distributor management, channel conflict resolution, trade marketing, and sales performance metrics.

subjects.name: Product and Brand Management
subjects.desc: Brand identity design, brand equity measurement (Keller, Aaker models), product lifecycle management, new product development process, and brand positioning strategy.

subjects.name: Retail Management
subjects.desc: Retail formats, store operations, category management, visual merchandising, retail pricing strategies, and omnichannel retail design.

subjects.name: Marketing of Services
subjects.desc: Services marketing 7Ps framework, SERVQUAL model, service blueprint design, customer retention, service recovery, and service brand differentiation.

subjects.name: Generic Elective-I: Digital Marketing or Business English
subjects.desc: Students select one. Digital Marketing adds SEO, social media, and performance marketing skills. Business English strengthens communication for marketing leadership.

subjects.name: International Marketing
subjects.desc: Global market entry strategies, international market research, cross-cultural marketing, international pricing, and global brand management.

subjects.name: Integrated Marketing Communication
subjects.desc: IMC planning, media mix optimisation, ATL-BTL integration, campaign measurement, PR and corporate communication, and event marketing.

subjects.name: Generic Elective-II: Soft Skills or Business Ethics and Corporate Governance
subjects.desc: Students select one. Soft Skills covers negotiation and leadership. Business Ethics covers corporate governance and ethical marketing.

subjects.name: Project Work Report
subjects.desc: Industry-linked marketing project. Typically a brand audit, consumer research study, IMC campaign plan, or retail operations analysis.

whoHires.body:
DPU-COL MBA Marketing Management graduates enter roles across FMCG, retail, services, and digital marketing:

**Industries:** FMCG, retail, banking (product marketing), telecom, e-commerce, media and entertainment, consumer durables, hospitality, and healthcare marketing

**Entry-level roles:** Marketing Executive, Brand Executive, Sales Executive, Retail Operations Executive, Market Research Analyst, Trade Marketing Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Manager, Marketing Manager, Regional Sales Manager, Category Manager (retail), Product Marketing Manager, Service Marketing Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Marketing, CMO, Head of Sales, National Sales Director, Head of Brand Management, Chief Commercial Officer

**Employers:** FMCG (HUL, ITC, P&G, Nestle, Britannia), retail (Reliance Retail, Trent, DMart, Shoppers Stop), e-commerce (Flipkart, Amazon India, Nykaa), banking (HDFC, ICICI product marketing), and telecom (Jio, Airtel, Vi marketing).

**Note:** DPU-COL provides placement assistance for all MBA programmes.

skills.body:
The Marketing Management specialisation builds skills across brand, sales, retail, and services marketing:

**Technical skills:**
- Brand Management: brand equity audit, brand positioning map, product lifecycle strategy, new product launch plan, and brand architecture design
- Marketing Research: survey design, focus group facilitation, data analysis (cross-tabulation, regression basics), and insights-to-action framework
- Sales and Distribution: sales territory design, distributor ROI calculation, trade scheme evaluation, and sales force productivity metrics
- Retail: category plan design, planogram basics, retail margin calculation, and store performance benchmarking

**Applied skills:**
- Services Marketing: service blueprint construction, SERVQUAL gap analysis, customer satisfaction survey design, and service recovery protocol
- IMC: media plan design, campaign ROI calculation, ATL-BTL budget allocation, and PR event management

**Soft skills:** Presenting marketing strategy to boards, negotiating with advertising agencies and media partners, managing cross-functional product launch teams, and leading brand turnaround initiatives.

comparisons.body:
Comparing DPU-COL Online MBA Marketing Management against peer programmes:

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Marketing:** CU at ₹1,65,000 with NIRF #19 and PwC co-teaching. CU wins on NIRF rank and fee. DPU at ₹1,89,400 holds NAAC A++ (vs CU A+) and has seven Sem 3 subjects covering consumer behaviour, marketing research, sales and distribution, brand management, retail, and services marketing as standalone courses.

**vs Amrita Online MBA Marketing:** Amrita at ₹2,20,000 with NIRF #8 and NAAC A++ is the premium option. DPU saves ₹30,600 with the same NAAC grade. Amrita wins on NIRF rank.

**vs LPU Online MBA Marketing:** LPU at approximately ₹2,00,000 with Coursera Plus. DPU saves ₹10,600 with stronger NAAC (A++ vs A+) and seven marketing subjects vs LPU's typical four to five.

**vs DPU-COL MBA Digital Marketing Management:** DPU's Digital Marketing spec focuses on SEO, social media, content, and web. Marketing Management covers the broader marketing spectrum: consumer behaviour, brand, sales, retail, services, and IMC. Choose Digital Marketing for digital-first roles. Choose Marketing Management for comprehensive marketing leadership.

fees.body:
The DPU-COL Online MBA in Marketing Management has a total programme fee of ₹1,89,400 for Indian students (USD 3,600 for international students).

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹50,000
- Semester 2: ₹50,000
- Semester 3: ₹45,000
- Semester 4: ₹44,400
- **Total: ₹1,89,400**

**Scholarship:** ₹10,000 off for full payment in one or two transactions within 15 days (effective fee ₹1,79,400).

**Payment options:**
- Zero-cost EMI: from ₹2,500/month directly through DPU-COL (no external NBFC, no interest)
- Semester-wise payments as above
- No application fee

**Bundled:** edX certifications included at no extra cost.

Fees are indicative. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com before payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Marketing Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune (DPU-COL) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #41 University rank. It is valid for government and private sector recruitment. edX certifications supplement the credential. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from DPU-COL Online MBA Marketing Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Ravi D.
items.liked: Consumer Behavior and Brand Management subjects gave me the conceptual framework I was applying intuitively after 5 years in FMCG sales
items.disliked: Digital marketing is only available as an elective — in 2024 it should be a core subject for any marketing MBA
items.body: I transitioned from a regional sales manager to a brand manager role at an FMCG company. Having seven subjects in Sem 3 meant the depth was genuinely better than the three or four-subject marketing programmes I evaluated. NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 met my employer's credential requirements.

items.name: Kavita S.
items.liked: Marketing of Services and Retail Management as standalone subjects is rare — most programmes bundle these into a single module
items.disliked: Marketing research module could include more tools training (SPSS, Qualtrics) alongside the theoretical methodology
items.body: I work in service marketing at a hospitality company. The services marketing, consumer behaviour, and IMC subjects were directly applicable. DPU being in Pune made the occasional workshop attendance convenient. The ₹10,000 lump-sum discount and edX certifications added practical value.

items.name: Amit P.
items.liked: Sales and Distribution Management covered territory design and distributor management at an operational level — directly useful for my FMCG distribution role
items.disliked: International Marketing content was traditional — more on D2C cross-border, Amazon Global Selling, and digital-first international market entry would modernise it
items.body: I manage distribution for an FMCG company across North India. The sales and distribution, retail management, and product management subjects strengthened my management vocabulary for internal assessments. NIRF #41 and NAAC A++ gave the MBA credential immediate acceptance. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month was manageable.

faqs.question: How many marketing subjects does this programme have compared to other online MBAs?
faqs.answer: DPU's Marketing Management has seven Semester 3 subjects and four Semester 4 subjects (including project and elective). Most competing online MBA marketing programmes have four to five total specialisation subjects. Consumer behaviour, marketing research, sales and distribution, brand management, retail, and services marketing are each standalone courses.

faqs.question: How does Marketing Management differ from DPU's Digital Marketing Management?
faqs.answer: Marketing Management covers the full marketing spectrum: consumer behaviour, brand, sales, retail, services, and IMC. Digital Marketing Management focuses on SEO, social media, content, web design, and digital campaign management. Choose Marketing Management for broad marketing leadership (brand manager, CMO track). Choose Digital Marketing for digital-first roles (digital marketing manager, SEO head).

faqs.question: Does the programme cover digital marketing?
faqs.answer: Digital Marketing is available as a Generic Elective-I option in Semester 3. It is not a core subject. Candidates who want digital marketing as the primary focus should consider DPU's dedicated Digital Marketing Management specialisation instead.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹1,89,400 total (Sem 1: ₹50,000, Sem 2: ₹50,000, Sem 3: ₹45,000, Sem 4: ₹44,400). ₹10,000 scholarship for upfront payment within 15 days. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. No application fee. edX bundled. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com.

faqs.question: Is this MBA valid for government and PSU marketing roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. DPU-COL's MBA is UGC-DEB approved from a NAAC A++ NIRF #41 university. It satisfies the MBA requirement for PSU marketing officer recruitment, government marketing board positions, and public sector enterprise commercial roles.

chooseIf: You want the deepest marketing curriculum among NAAC A++ online MBAs — seven Sem 3 subjects covering consumer behaviour, brand, sales, retail, services, and research.
Your target is broad marketing leadership (brand manager, CMO track, sales director) rather than digital marketing specifically.
₹1,89,400 with zero-cost EMI and edX certifications fits your budget at NAAC A++ level.
You work in FMCG, retail, services, or B2B sales and need comprehensive marketing management depth.
consider: NIRF top 20 matters — CU (NIRF #19) at ₹1,65,000 or Amrita (NIRF #8) at ₹2,20,000 are stronger on rank.
Your career target is digital marketing specifically — DPU's Digital Marketing Management spec is more focused.
You need the most affordable marketing MBA — IGNOU Marketing Management at ₹66,000 is the lowest-cost NAAC A++ option.
